Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to participate in neoxanthin formation upstream of ABA biosynthesis, but gene identity/function remained unresolved in this review
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ready for deployment because enzymatic identity was unresolved
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSY was proposed as an enzyme for neoxanthin formation upstream of ABA biosynthesis. 2. Putative tomato NSY was later shown to encode an LCYB isoform. 3. The true Arabidopsis NSY/isomerase gene had not yet been definitively established in this review.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because repeatedly discussed as a core unresolved step; not definitively validated at the time of review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutations confer ABA-deficient phenotypes through impaired MoCo sulfurase activity needed for aldehyde oxidase
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA deficiency-associated wilting and developmental effects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Functional ortholog information useful for comparative engineering of terminal ABA synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLACCA encodes a MoCo sulfurase in tomato. 2. Mutations cause ABA-deficient phenotypes. 3. It is functionally analogous to Arabidopsis ABA3 in activating aldehyde oxidase.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as orthologous support for cofactor activation requirement.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reverse reaction competes with violaxanthin accumulation and can influence precursor availability for ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential flux-balancing target upstream of ABA precursor formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VDE catalyzes the reverse reaction from violaxanthin to zeaxanthin in chloroplasts under high light. 2. This step can affect the precursor pool for ABA biosynthesis. 3. It is discussed as contextual pathway component rather than a major focal gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background pathway gene included for completeness of metabolism logic.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana plumbaginifolia; Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana plumbaginifolia; Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: epoxy-carotenoid synthesis / xanthophyll cycle upstream of ABA branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes zeaxanthin to violaxanthin via antheraxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls seed dormancy, germination, stomatal closure, dehydration and osmotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by drought stress in roots of Nicotiana plumbaginifolia, tomato, and Arabidopsis; expressed in testa and young embryos in Arabidopsis; peaks earlier during seed development in Nicotiana plumbaginifolia
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis induced by dehydration and glucose; not induced by dehydration in leaves in some reports; expressed in testa and young embryos
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dehydration; osmotic stress; glucose; seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; mutant analysis; overexpression; gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function causes zeaxanthin accumulation, severe ABA reduction, wilty phenotype, and nondormant seeds; overexpression can enhance ABA-related stress responses in some contexts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: albino/wilty phenotypes and reduced dormancy in mutants; overexpression may show context-dependent effects on stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target to increase ABA, but effects are context dependent and may require stacking with downstream ABA-pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZEP catalyzes zeaxanthin epoxidation to violaxanthin upstream of ABA formation. 2. zep mutants in several species have low ABA, wilty plants, and nondormant seeds. 3. ZEP overexpression can increase ABA-related responses in some seedling or stress contexts but is not consistently sufficient for drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reverse reaction to zeaxanthin in high light is catalyzed by VDE; review notes focus is downstream of xanthophyll formation.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: VP14; AtNCED2; AtNCED3; AtNCED5; AtNCED6; AtNCED9; LeNCED1; PvNCED1
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Phaseolus vulgaris; Nicotiana plumbaginifolia
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Arabidopsis thaliana; Solanum lycopersicum; Phaseolus vulgaris; Nicotiana plumbaginifolia
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family; non-heme iron-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-violaxanthin and 9'-cis-neoxanthin to produce xanthoxin and a C25 apocarotenoid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls seed maturation, dormancy, germination, stomatal closure, and stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: vascular bundles; guard cells; seeds; leaves; roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AtNCED3 strongly induced by dehydration; AtNCED2/3 expressed in guard cells; AtNCED5/6 highly expressed in young to mid/late embryos; PvNCED1 induced in leaves and roots by water stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dry and developing seed expression varies by family member; stress induction strongest for AtNCED3 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dehydration; drought; osmotic stress; seed development; imbibition; exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutagenesis; recombinant enzyme assay; mutant analysis; overexpression; transcript and protein profiling; inhibitor studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA accumulation; mutants show ABA deficiency phenotypes though often mild because of gene redundancy; overexpression increases ABA and desiccation tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; proposed regulatory enzyme and committed step
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Gene redundancy may mask phenotypes; increased ABA may enhance dormancy and alter growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering drought tolerance and seed traits; overexpression increases ABA and stress tolerance, but effects on dormancy/growth should be balanced
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED catalyzes cleavage of cis-xanthophylls to xanthoxin, the committed step in ABA biosynthesis. 2. VP14 and related NCED genes are core determinants of ABA accumulation across species. 3. Overexpression of AtNCED3, LeNCED1, or PvNCED1 raises ABA and improves desiccation tolerance, whereas mutants show ABA-deficient phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NCED is a multigene family; only certain members contribute to ABA biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. Recombinant VP14 does not cleave trans-xanthophylls.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of ABA final oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls seed dormancy, germination, and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in vascular bundles and funicules; not induced by osmotic stress; induced by glucose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Observed in vascular bundles of turgid tissues and funicules of newly fertilized ovules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; mutant alleles; domain mutation analysis; intragenic complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single-gene loss of function causes severe ABA deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ABA deficiency in mutants likely affects dormancy and water relations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for modulating ABA content, though severe pleiotropy expected from strong loss-of-function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtABA2 encodes the SDR enzyme converting xanthoxin to abscisic aldehyde. 2. It is encoded by a single gene in Arabidopsis, so loss of function causes severe ABA deficiency. 3. Functional domains and likely multimeric structure are important for activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutations map to NAD-binding, catalytic, subunit interaction, and substrate-binding domains; intragenic complementation suggests multimerization.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: final oxidation step of C15 cytosolic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes abscisic aldehyde to abscisic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: regulates dormancy, germination, stomatal function, and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: vascular bundles; phloem companion cells; xylem parenchyma; guard cells; seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in vascular bundles of turgid tissues; induced by dehydration, glucose, and osmotic stress; induced in guard cells upon stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein localized abundantly in phloem companion cells, xylem parenchyma cells, and guard cells
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dehydration; osmotic stress; glucose; guard-cell stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis including null alleles; localization with AAO3-GFP; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for final ABA production; mutants show wilty phenotype and ABA-deficient seed phenotypes, with null alleles confirming major role in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants are wilt-prone; altered ABA may affect seed dormancy and drought responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning terminal ABA synthesis and tissue-specific ABA production, particularly vascular and guard-cell responses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAO3 catalyzes the final oxidation of abscisic aldehyde to ABA. 2. Null alleles show AAO3 is most likely the only aldehyde oxidase involved in Arabidopsis ABA biosynthesis. 3. AAO3 is localized in vascular tissues and guard cells, linking local ABA synthesis to physiology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Requires sulfurated molybdenum cofactor for activity; early mild seed phenotype in aao3-1 was later explained by allele strength rather than true redundancy.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cofactor activation for AAO3-dependent terminal oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates molybdenum cofactor required for aldehyde oxidase activity by sulfurylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for terminal ABA-forming oxidation by AAO3
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls dormancy and abiotic stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by dehydration and osmotic stress in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of ABA biosynthesis through AAO activation; mutants are ABA deficient
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA-deficient phenotypes affect water relations and dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered for engineering stronger terminal-step ABA synthesis together with AAO3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtABA3 encodes a MoCo sulfurase required to activate aldehyde oxidase for ABA biosynthesis. 2. Mutations in AtABA3 cause ABA-deficient phenotypes. 3. AtABA3 expression is induced under osmotic stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato ortholog FLACCA has analogous function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ABA at the 8' methyl group to form 8'-hydroxy ABA, which spontaneously isomerizes to phaseic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA controls dormancy, germination, and stress responses; CYP707A reduces active ABA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All four genes induced by osmotic stress and dehydration; CYP707A3 induced by gibberellin and brassinolide; CYP707A1 and CYP707A3 expressed in mid-stage seed development; CYP707A2 high in dry seed and early imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CYP707A2 increases within 6 h of imbibition and then decreases; multiple genes induced strongly upon rehydration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dehydration; rehydration; salinity; osmotic stress; seed imbibition; gibberellin; brassinolide; exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reverse genetics; recombinant enzyme assay; mutant analysis; inhibitor assays; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA catabolism and dormancy release; cyp707a2 causes hyperdormancy and elevated ABA in dry/imbibed seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; committed step in predominant catabolic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced catabolism may reduce stress tolerance; reduced catabolism can cause hyperdormancy
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target to manipulate dormancy, germination, and stress recovery; allele-specific or tissue-specific modulation is preferable to avoid excess dormancy or stress susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis CYP707A genes encode ABA 8'-hydroxylases. 2. They catalyze the committed step in the predominant ABA catabolic pathway. 3. CYP707A2 is crucial for rapid ABA decline during seed imbibition and dormancy release; family members are also induced by dehydration/rehydration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recombinant enzyme produces 8'-hydroxy ABA but not 7' or 9' hydroxylated catabolites; tetracyclasis inhibits activity whereas metyrapone does not.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates ABA with UDP-D-glucose to form ABA glucosyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: inactive/storage or transport form implicated in long-distance transport and allelopathy
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by dehydration and wounding in adzuki bean hypocotyls but not leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene identification; recombinant protein enzyme assay; substrate specificity analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA conjugation to ABA-GE, potentially reducing free ABA while creating transport/storage form
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate specificity may affect other small molecules; may not conjugate PA/DPA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ABA homeostasis or conjugate pools, but tissue specificity and substrate breadth should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOG is the first reported gene for ABA catabolism via glucosylation. 2. Recombinant AOG conjugates ABA with UDP-glucose to produce ABA-GE. 3. AOG has broad substrate specificity and is induced by dehydration and wounding in hypocotyls.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not glucosylate PA; PA/DPA glucosylation likely requires different enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: putative fungal ABA biosynthetic cytochrome P450
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed oxidative step(s) in the direct fungal ABA biosynthetic pathway from sesquiterpenoid intermediates to ABA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ABA pathway oxidized intermediate(s) leading to ABA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: may enhance plant susceptibility by suppressing salicylic acid-dependent defense
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression associated with ABA production in Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene inactivation; expression association with ABA production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function abolishes ABA accumulation in Botrytis cinerea
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reduce fungal ABA-mediated virulence interactions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcABA1 is the first fungal ABA biosynthetic gene identified. 2. Its expression correlates with ABA production in Botrytis cinerea. 3. Loss of function abolishes fungal ABA accumulation, supporting a direct fungal ABA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review describes fungal pathway as distinct from higher plants and likely involving direct cyclization of FDP derivatives.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA accumulation during embryogenesis and seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ABA level positively; gibberellin synthesis negatively
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates ABA levels and downregulates GA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: prevents vivipary, promotes seed maturation and dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA accumulation to prevent vivipary and support embryo growth arrest
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: developmental regulator linking high ABA and low GA to seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered embryo growth arrest and vivipary when defective
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential developmental target for dormancy control, though likely pleiotropic due to broad seed developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUS3 positively regulates ABA levels during seed development. 2. It also downregulates GA synthesis, helping prevent vivipary. 3. fus3 mutants reveal the importance of developmental regulation of ABA metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Maternal ABA can partially inhibit vivipary in fus3 mutants.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive regulator of AAO3 and AtABA3 expression/mRNA stability-related control
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates drought and stress responses; PA is ABA catabolite
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possibly involved in feedback on ABA metabolism and signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic analysis of sad1 mutant; expression and feeding analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: sad1 mutant shows reduced ABA and PA levels, indicating positive regulation of ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: post-transcriptional positive regulator linking RNA processing to ABA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutant is supersensitive to ABA and drought while metabolite levels are reduced, indicating complex signaling-metabolism crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights RNA processing as a lever for ABA homeostasis, but utility may be limited by pleiotropic effects on signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAD1 encodes an Sm-like protein involved in mRNA processing. 2. It positively regulates AAO3 and AtABA3 and thereby ABA biosynthesis. 3. sad1 mutants have reduced ABA and PA, showing post-transcriptional control of ABA metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes RNA processing genes as tightly connected to ABA metabolism and signaling.
